result:
All six Phase CRDs are well-formed and correct. The work is complete.

## Summary

I read the TIDE spec (`README.md`), studied the existing foundation (`internal/subagent/anthropic/`, `pkg/dispatch.{Subagent,ProviderSpec}`, `ResolveProvider`/`resolveImage` in `dispatch_helpers.go`, `SubagentConfig`/`LevelConfig`, `cmd/claude-subagent`, chart values, pricing tables), then authored the milestone and decomposed it.

### MILESTONE.md (worktree repo root, replacing the prior-milestone example)
**Milestone 03 — Heterogeneous Provider Dispatch (Codex Runtime)**, slug `milestone-03-hetero-integration`. Declarative, spec-voiced doc with: a single user-visible outcome statement (operator runs Claude planners + Codex executors in one mixed-provider run to completion), six locked architecture decisions (D1 mirror-anthropic layering, D2 per-level vendor switch, D3 Secret-ref/env-key creds, D4 unchanged wave-boundary failure semantics, D5 required usage/cost normalization, D6 verbatim Codex CLI facts), the phase DAG (mermaid), exit criteria, inherited constraints, and upstream/downstream linkage.

### Six Phase child-CRDs (`children/*.json`)
Each is `{kind:"Phase", name, spec:{milestoneRef, dependsOn}}` wired to this milestone:

| Phase | dependsOn | Wave |
|-------|-----------|------|
| phase-01-codex-pricing-usage | — | 1 |
| phase-04-per-level-vendor-switch | — | 1 |
| phase-02-codex-subagent-core | 01 | 2 |
| phase-05-chart-secret-wiring | 04 | 2 |
| phase-03-codex-image-entrypoint | 02 | 3 |
| phase-06-hetero-integration | 02, 03, 04, 05 | 4 |

Each phase has a clear deliverable boundary; cross-phase contracts (the `"openai"` sentinel, `Usage` mapping, vendor precedence chain, image name, wave-failure contract) are locked in the doc so the planning DAG stays shallow — phases 01/04 fan out in parallel, and 06 is the join barrier. The dependency declarations are acyclic and reference only earlier siblings within this milestone.

Note: a couple of tooling constraints surfaced — the Bash sandbox blocked heredocs containing brace-quote JSON and required approval for `python3`/`node` execution, so I authored the JSON children via the Edit tool (empty `old_string` creates new files) and verified them by reading them back.
